study_title: Effects of Resistance Training Frequency on Muscle Hypertrophy
journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
key_finding: Training each muscle group 2-3 times per week yields superior hypertrophy compared to once per week.

study_title: Protein Timing and Muscle Protein Synthesis: A Meta-Analysis
key_finding: Total daily protein intake is more important than immediate post-workout consumption for muscle growth.

study_title: The Impact of Sleep Duration on Athletic Performance
key_finding: Athletes sleeping less than 7 hours per night exhibit slower reaction times and reduced accuracy.

study_title: Foam Rolling and Delayed-Onset Muscle Soreness: A Systematic Review
key_finding: Foam rolling reduces perceived DOMS by 20-30% without impairing subsequent performance.

study_title: Caffeine Dose-Response for Anaerobic Performance
key_finding: 3-6 mg/kg caffeine 30-60 min pre-exercise improves peak power output by 5-8%.

study_title: High-Intensity Interval Training vs. Moderate Continuous Training for Fat Loss
key_finding: HIIT and MICT result in similar fat loss when energy expenditure is matched; HIIT is more time-efficient.

study_title: Safety of Creatine Supplementation in Healthy Adults: A Systematic Review
key_finding: Creatine monohydrate at 3-5 g/day is safe for long-term use with no adverse renal effects.

study_title: Dynamic vs. Static Stretching on Power Output
journal: Scandinavian Journal of Medicine & Science in Sports
key_finding: Dynamic stretching before exercise enhances power, while static stretching may temporarily reduce it.

study_title: Resistance Training for Bone Health in Postmenopausal Women
key_finding: Progressive resistance training 2x/week increases lumbar spine BMD by 2-3% over 12 months.

study_title: Effects of Hydration Status on Cognitive Function in Athletes
journal: Journal of the International Society of Sports Nutrition
key_finding: Dehydration of 2% body mass impairs attention and short-term memory during prolonged exercise.

study_title: Nutritional Strategies for Endurance Athletes: A Practical Review
key_finding: Carbohydrate intake of 6-10 g/kg/day supports high-volume training; race-day fueling with 30-90 g/h.

study_title: Overtraining Syndrome: Pathophysiology and Diagnosis
key_finding: Persistent fatigue, performance decline, and mood disturbances beyond 3 weeks indicate overtraining; rest is primary treatment.

study_title: Benefits of Yoga for Flexibility and Balance in Healthy Adults
key_finding: Twice-weekly yoga improves sit-and-reach scores by 4-6 cm and single-leg stance time by 25%.

study_title: Resistance Training for Youth: Updated Position Stand
journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
key_finding: Supervised resistance training 2-3x/week is safe and effective for children and adolescents, improving strength by 30-50%.

study_title: The Role of Omega-3 Fatty Acids in Muscle Recovery
key_finding: Omega-3 supplementation (3-5 g/day) reduces muscle soreness and may enhance protein synthesis in older adults.

study_title: Inter-Set Rest Periods and Hypertrophy: A Dose-Response
key_finding: 3-minute rest intervals produce greater hypertrophy and strength gains than 1-minute rests.

study_title: Resistance Training for Type 2 Diabetes Management
key_finding: Resistance training 2-3x/week reduces HbA1c by 0.5-0.8% and improves insulin sensitivity.

study_title: Music and Exercise Performance: A Meta-Analysis
key_finding: Music during exercise improves endurance by 15% and reduces perceived exertion; high-tempo music most effective.

study_title: Cold Water Immersion for Recovery: A Systematic Review
journal: Journal of Science and Medicine in Sport
key_finding: CWI at 10-15°C for 10-15 min reduces DOMS but may attenuate long-term strength adaptations if used chronically.

study_title: Comparison of Free Weights vs. Machines for Strength Gains
journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
key_finding: Free weights and machines produce similar strength gains in untrained individuals; free weights superior for experienced lifters due to stabilizer activation.

study_title: Breakfast Skipping and Exercise Performance
journal: International Journal of Sport Nutrition and Exercise Metabolism
key_finding: Skipping breakfast before morning exercise increases fat oxidation but may impair high-intensity performance.

study_title: Mobility Training for Injury Prevention in Runners
journal: Journal of Orthopaedic & Sports Physical Therapy
key_finding: A 15-min daily mobility routine reduces running-related injuries by 30% over 6 months.

study_title: Effect of Sleep Extension on Athletic Performance
key_finding: Extending sleep to 10 hours per night for 6 weeks improved sprint times and shooting accuracy in basketball players.

study_title: The Anabolic Window: Myth or Reality?
journal: Journal of the International Society of Sports Nutrition
key_finding: The post-exercise anabolic window is wider than previously thought; total daily protein and energy intake are primary.

study_title: Effects of Heat Therapy on Muscle Recovery
key_finding: Post-exercise sauna (80°C, 15 min) increases blood flow and may reduce DOMS; hydration critical.

study_title: Long-Term Effects of Ketogenic Diets on Strength Athletes
journal: Journal of the International Society of Sports Nutrition
key_finding: A 12-week ketogenic diet preserved strength but reduced high-intensity exercise capacity compared to a moderate-carb diet.

study_title: Biomechanics of the Squat: Implications for Injury Prevention
journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
key_finding: Knee tracking over toes is safe; lumbar flexion under load is the primary injury risk.

study_title: Practical Strategies for Weight Loss Maintenance in Athletes
key_finding: High-protein diet, regular weigh-ins, and cognitive behavioral strategies are most effective for long-term weight maintenance.

study_title: Exercise and Immunity: The Open Window Theory
key_finding: Moderate exercise boosts immune function; prolonged intense exercise (>90 min) temporarily suppresses immunity for 3-24 hours.

study_title: The Effect of Intermittent Fasting on Resistance Training Outcomes
key_finding: Intermittent fasting can preserve muscle mass during weight loss when combined with resistance training; meal timing less critical than total intake.
tags: intermittent fasting; resistance training; body composition
